zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Marguerite DU BOIS DE FIENNES于大约1025年出生在法国豪-德-福朗斯帕-德-卡拉斯範恩斯时，她的父亲Hendrik I van Leuven-Brussel32岁，母亲Mathilde de Lorraine35岁。她和Baudoin DE CREQUY至少有2个儿子和3个女儿。

nameMeanings.name: DU
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Hong, Jian, Jing, Li, Ping, Yan, Yang, Yong, Bin, Chan, Cheng.  Vietnamese Thanh, Quang, Cuong, Dung, Hung, Binh, Minh, Duong, Hao, Hien, Liem, Lien.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 杜, meaning ‘birchleaf pear’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by descendants of Du Kang (杜康), an official noted as a winemaker during the reign of the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (ii) from the placename Du (杜), the name of a city (located in Shaanxi province) granted to Du Bo (杜伯, the Earl of Du), head of the state of Du (杜), who was eventually killed by King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782 BC ). After that, the state of Du (杜) was annexed and the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (iii) adopted as a surname by the Du Gu Hun (獨孤渾) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 都, meaning ‘capital’ in Chinese: (i) from the personal name Zi Du (子都), style name of Gong-Sun Yu, an official in the state of Zheng during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) shortened form of the compound surname Gong Du (公都), which is sometimes traced back to Tian, a prince in the state of Chu who was granted the fief Du (都), or alternatively to Gong Du Zi (公都子), an official in the state of Qi.

variants.name: Doo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Jin Ho, Young, Jong, Ling, Mi Sook, Quong, Yi.
Chinese: variant of   Du  .
English (Surrey): probably a variant of   Dew  .

variants.name: Duh
meanings: Slovenian and Croatian: nickname from duh ‘spirit, ghost’.
Chinese: variant of   Du  .

variants.name: Do
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Minh, Thanh, Hien, Long, Dung, Anh, Tuan, Hoang, Hai, Quang, Son.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Chi, Sang, Dong, Chan, Han, Ho, Bai, Chien, Lai, Man.
Vietnamese (Đỗ): from the Chinese surname 杜, see Du 1.
Vietnamese (Đồ): from the Chinese surname 涂 (or 塗), see Tu 1.

variants.name: Dou
meanings: Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 竇, meaning ‘hole’ in ancient Chinese: (i) adopted as a surname by Yue Zhu and Yue Long, two sons of Shao Kang, the sixth king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ). Yue Zhu and Yue Long are said to have adopted Dou (竇) (meaning ‘hole’) as a surname because their pregnant grandmother had escaped from a rebellion through a hole in the wall before giving birth to their father, Shao Kang, who later recaptured the throne. (ii) traced back to the Dou (竇) family from the Di ethnic group and some other families from the Xianbei ethnic group in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 豆, denoting an ancient stemmed cup of bowl: (i) traced back to some inhabitants in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) adopted as the surname by the Chi Xiao Dou (赤小豆) and Dou Lu (豆盧) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 鈄, referring to a type of ancient vessel for drinking wine: said to be borne by the descendants of the Duke Kang of Qi (died 379  BC ), who was ousted from his throne in the state of Qi and sent into exile. It was said that he was forced to use the vessel Dou (鈄) to cook food during his exile, and some of his descendants inherited Dou as the surname to memorize this period.

variants.name: Yu
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Ming, Yong, Young, Ping, Chi, Chun, Jae, Li, Sung, Jin, Yang.  Korean Chung, Chang, Byung, Chong, Min, Pyong, Myong, Hyong, Moon, Yiu, Inyoung, Shiu.

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 余 (or rarely written as 餘), meaning ‘I’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the second element of the personal name You Yu (由余), the name of a prime minister in the state of Qin during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from Yu (余), the name of a small state during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) said to be borne by descendants of Xia Yu, the first king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 于 (meaning ‘go’ in ancient Chinese) or 於 (often simplified as 于 in Mainland China, meaning ‘depend on’ in ancient Chinese): (i) simplified form of the Chinese character 邘, the name of an ancient state (located in Qinyang in Henan province), which was granted to Yu Shu (邘叔), the third son of King Wen of Zhou (1152–1056 BC ). After it was annexed by the state of Zheng during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ), the Chinese character 邘 (pronounced Yu) was simplified to (于) and adopted as a surname. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Wan Niu Yu (萬忸于) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ). (iii) adopted in place of the Chinese compound surname Chun-Yu (淳于), because the first element of the surname, Chun, happened to share the same pronunciation with the personal name of the emperor Li Chun, also known as Emperor Xianzong of Tang (778–820 AD ), which was taboo in ancient China. (iv) said to be traced back to Yu Ze (於則), who said to be the inventor of shoes, an official the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (v) from the second element of the placename Shang Yu (商於) in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ).

nameMeanings.name: BOIS
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alcide, Antoine, Donat, Marcel, Ovide, Ovila, Raoul, Serge.

French: topographic name for someone living or working in a wood, from (Old) French bois ‘wood’ (from Late Latin boscum ‘bush, shrub, undergrowth’, of ancient Germanic origin), or a habitational name from (Le) Bois, the name of several places in various parts of France. This surname is also found in Haiti. Compare Debois and the most frequent French form Dubois . Compare also Boie , Boies , and Boyce .
English: variant of   Boyce   and   Boyes  .

variants.name: Dubois
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Normand, Jacques, Pierre, Fernand, Marcel, Emile, Lucien, Michel, Alain, Luc.

French, Walloon, and English (of French Huguenot origin): topographic name, with fused preposition and definite article du ‘from the’, for someone living or working in a wood (see Bois ), or a habitational name for someone from any of several places in various parts of France and in Belgium (Wallonia) named Le Bois. This is the most frequent surname in Wallonia. It is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium, the Netherlands, Germany, and Haiti. In both England and North America it is sometimes translated into English as Wood . Compare Deboe , Debois , Deboy , Duboise , and Dubose .

History: This surname is listed (in the form Du Bois) in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America. One of the several Huguenot ancestors bearing this name was Chrétien Du Bois from Flanders, whose son, Louis Du Bois (born 1626 in Lille, France, died 1696 in Kingston, NY), was among the Huguenots who founded the town of New Paltz, NY 1675–78.

variants.name: Boisvert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Armand, Andre, Donat, Lucien, Jacques, Gaston, Normand, Pierre, Gilles, Herve, Adrien.
French:
topographic name for someone who lived in a dense forest or perhaps a copse of evergreens, from Old French bois ‘wood’ (see Bois ) + vert, verd ‘green’.

variants.name: Boisclair
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Normand, Marcel, Aldege, Alphonse, Arsene, Edmour, Emile, Leopaul, Prudent, Rejean, Remi, Yves.

French: topographic name from Old French bois ‘wood’ (see Bois ) + clair ‘clear, light’, or a habitational name from (Le) Bois Clair, the name of several places in various parts of France. This surname is virtually non-existing in France.

variants.name: Boies
meanings: Altered form of French   Bois  .

variants.name: Baskerville
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Boscherville in Eure, France, named with Old Anglo-Norman French boschet ‘copse, thicket’ (a diminutive of Bois ) + ville ‘settlement, town’.

variants.name: Debois
meanings: Altered form of French   Dubois  , a cognate of 1 above.
French: topographic name, with fused preposition de ‘from’, for someone living or working in a wood (see Bois ).

nameMeanings.name: DE
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: FIENNES
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English:  see  Finnis  .

variants.name: Finnis
meanings: Norman, English, Irish: locative name from Fiennes (Pas-de-Calais).  See also  Filtness  .

nameMeanings.name: Marguerite
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of Margaret , also used in the English-speaking world, where its use has been reinforced by the fact that the name was adopted in the 19th century for a garden flower, a large cultivated variety of daisy. Margaret was earlier used in English as a dialect word denoting the ox-eye daisy, and the French equivalent was borrowed into English just in time to catch the vogue for deriving girls' names from vocabulary words denoting flowers. See also Daisy .

en.lifeSummary: When Marguerite DU BOIS DE FIENNES was born about 1025, in Fiennes, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, France, her father, Hendrik I van Leuven-Brussel, was 32 and her mother, Mathilde de Lorraine, was 35. She had at least 2 sons and 3 daughters with Baudoin DE CREQUY.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Marguerite DU BOIS DE FIENNES est née environ 1025, à Fiennes, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, France, son père, Hendrik I van Leuven-Brussel, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Mathilde de Lorraine, avait 35 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles avec Baudoin DE CREQUY.

de.lifeSummary: Als Marguerite DU BOIS DE FIENNES um 1025 in Fiennes, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Hendrik I van Leuven-Brussel 32 und ihre Mutter Mathilde de Lorraine 35. Sie hatte mit Baudoin DE CREQUY mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Marguerite DU BOIS DE FIENNES è nata il circa 1025 nel Fiennes, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, Francia, sua padre, Hendrik I van Leuven-Brussel, aveva 32 anni e sua madre, Mathilde de Lorraine, aveva 35 anni. Lei avuto almeno 2 figli e 3 figlie con Baudoin DE CREQUY.

ja.Lorraine, Holy Roman Empire: ヘイリジェズ ルoミシェス レイチロスリンジェン
ja.lifeSummary: Marguerite DU BOIS DE FIENNESが約1025年にフランスハウトス-デ-フランスプアス-デ-カレイスフィーンスで生まれた時，父Hendrik I van Leuven-Brusselは32歳，母Mathilde de Lorraineは35歳であった。彼女はBaudoin DE CREQUYとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Marguerite DU BOIS DE FIENNES(이)가 대략 1025년 프랑스 하츠-데-프랜스 파스-데-캘레이 피네스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Hendrik I van Leuven-Brussel(은)는 32세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mathilde de Lorraine(은)는 35세였다. 그녀는 Baudoin DE CREQUY(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Marguerite DU BOIS DE FIENNES nasceu aproximadamente 1025, em Fiennes, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, França, seu pai, Hendrik I van Leuven-Brussel, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Mathilde de Lorraine, tinha 35 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 3 filhas com Baudoin DE CREQUY.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Marguerite DU BOIS DE FIENNES родилась около 1025 г. в местности фиеннесь, пас-де-калеи, хауц-де-фрэнс, Франция, её отцу по имени Hendrik I van Leuven-Brussel было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Mathilde de Lorraine, 35. У неё было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери от Baudoin DE CREQUY.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Marguerite DU BOIS DE FIENNES nació aproximadamente 1025, en Fiennes, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France, Francia, su papá, Hendrik I van Leuven-Brussel, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Mathilde de Lorraine, tenía 35 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos y 3 hijas con Baudoin DE CREQUY.
